_definition.ISO_639_3_eng: The shape property for a single, indivisible value. When the object is empty, the descriptor accepts any scalar value without type constraints. When non-empty, the required [`_type_scalar`](_type_scalar.md) property determines the data type and governs which additional properties are permitted.

_description.ISO_639_3_eng:
A *scalar* is a single, indivisible datum ‚Äî a number, a boolean, a string, or a reference to another term. It contrasts with the compound shapes ([`_array`](_array.md), [`_set`](_set.md), [`_tuple`](_tuple.md), [`_dict`](_dict.md)), which hold multiple values.

When [`_scalar`](_scalar.md) is an *empty object* (`{}`), the descriptor accepts any scalar value of any type, with no further constraints.

When non-empty, [`_type_scalar`](_type_scalar.md) is required and governs the entire property configuration. Each type value activates a conditional rule that defines exactly which additional properties are permitted. [`_unit`](_unit.md) and the free-text pair [`_unit-name`](_unit-name.md) / [`_unit-symbol`](_unit-symbol.md) are mutually exclusive ‚Äî use the codified unit when available, fall back to the free-text pair otherwise.

| [`_type_scalar`](_type_scalar.md) | Description | Companion properties |
|---|---|---|
| [`_type_boolean`](_type_boolean.md) | True/false boolean value. | ‚Äî |
| [`_type_number`](_type_number.md) | Number; accepts both integers and floating-point values. | [`_decimals`](_decimals.md); [`_unit`](_unit.md) / [`_unit-name`](_unit-name.md) / [`_unit-symbol`](_unit-symbol.md); [`_valid-range`](_valid-range.md) / [`_normal-range`](_normal-range.md) |
| [`_type_number_float`](_type_number_float.md) | Floating-point number. | [`_decimals`](_decimals.md); [`_unit`](_unit.md) / [`_unit-name`](_unit-name.md) / [`_unit-symbol`](_unit-symbol.md); [`_valid-range`](_valid-range.md) / [`_normal-range`](_normal-range.md) |
| [`_type_number_integer`](_type_number_integer.md) | Integer ‚Äî no decimal part. | [`_unit`](_unit.md) / [`_unit-name`](_unit-name.md) / [`_unit-symbol`](_unit-symbol.md); [`_valid-range`](_valid-range.md) / [`_normal-range`](_normal-range.md) |
| [`_type_string`](_type_string.md) | Generic UTF-8 string. | [`_regexp`](_regexp.md); [`_unit`](_unit.md) / [`_unit-name`](_unit-name.md) / [`_unit-symbol`](_unit-symbol.md); [`_valid-range_string`](_valid-range_string.md) / [`_normal-range_string`](_normal-range_string.md) |
| [`_type_string_Markdown`](_type_string_Markdown.md) | Markdown-formatted text. | ‚Äî |
| [`_type_string_HTML`](_type_string_HTML.md) | HTML-formatted text. | ‚Äî |
| [`_type_string_URI`](_type_string_URI.md) | Uniform Resource Identifier. | ‚Äî |
| [`_type_string_HEX`](_type_string_HEX.md) | Hexadecimal string. | [`_unit`](_unit.md) / [`_unit-name`](_unit-name.md) / [`_unit-symbol`](_unit-symbol.md); [`_valid-range_string`](_valid-range_string.md) / [`_normal-range_string`](_normal-range_string.md) |
| [`_type_string_SVG`](_type_string_SVG.md) | SVG image data. | ‚Äî |
| [`_type_string_email`](_type_string_email.md) | Email address. | ‚Äî |
| [`_type_string_date`](_type_string_date.md) | Date in JSON Schema `date` format (`YYYY-MM-DD`). | [`_valid-range_string`](_valid-range_string.md) / [`_normal-range_string`](_normal-range_string.md) |
| [`_type_string_time`](_type_string_time.md) | Time in JSON Schema `time` format. | [`_valid-range_string`](_valid-range_string.md) / [`_normal-range_string`](_normal-range_string.md) |
| [`_type_string_date-time`](_type_string_date-time.md) | Date-time in JSON Schema `date-time` format. | [`_valid-range_string`](_valid-range_string.md) / [`_normal-range_string`](_normal-range_string.md) |
| [`_type_string_YMD`](_type_string_YMD.md) | Partial or full date in YYYYMMDD format (YYYY, YYYYMM, or YYYYMMDD). | [`_valid-range_string`](_valid-range_string.md) / [`_normal-range_string`](_normal-range_string.md) |
| [`_type_string_Hostname`](_type_string_Hostname.md) | Internet hostname. | ‚Äî |
| [`_type_string_IPv4`](_type_string_IPv4.md) | IPv4 address. | ‚Äî |
| [`_type_string_IPv6`](_type_string_IPv6.md) | IPv6 address. | ‚Äî |
| [`_type_string_LaTeX`](_type_string_LaTeX.md) | LaTeX expression; rendered with KaTeX. | ‚Äî |
| [`_type_string_regexp`](_type_string_regexp.md) | Regular expression; the stored value is itself a pattern. | ‚Äî |
| [`_type_key`](_type_key.md) | `_key` of any ArangoDB document. | ‚Äî |
| [`_type_key_term`](_type_key_term.md) | `_key` of any term. | ‚Äî |
| [`_type_key_term_enum`](_type_key_term_enum.md) | `_key` of an enumeration root ‚Äî the root of a controlled vocabulary. | ‚Äî |
| [`_type_key_term_enum_element`](_type_key_term_enum_element.md) | `_key` of an enumeration element ‚Äî a valid choice within a controlled vocabulary. | ‚Äî |
| [`_type_key_term_descriptor`](_type_key_term_descriptor.md) | `_key` of a descriptor (has a [`_data`](_data.md) section). | ‚Äî |
| [`_type_key_term_object`](_type_key_term_object.md) | `_key` of an object definition term (has a [`_rule`](_rule.md) section). | ‚Äî |
| [`_type_key_term_predicate`](_type_key_term_predicate.md) | `_key` of a predicate term. | ‚Äî |
| [`_type_handle`](_type_handle.md) | ArangoDB document handle (`<collection>/<_key>`). | ‚Äî |
| [`_type_enum`](_type_enum.md) | `_gid` of an enumeration element ‚Äî a value drawn from a controlled vocabulary. | [`_kind_enum`](_kind_enum.md) |
| [`_type_object`](_type_object.md) | Object whose properties are descriptor `_gid`s; may be empty. | [`_kind_object`](_kind_object.md) |
| [`_type_struct`](_type_struct.md) | Object with indeterminate properties; may be empty. | ‚Äî |
| [`_type_timestamp`](_type_timestamp.md) | Unix timestamp (integer seconds since 1970-01-01 UTC). | [`_valid-range`](_valid-range.md) / [`_normal-range`](_normal-range.md) |
| [`_type_object_GeoJSON`](_type_object_GeoJSON.md) | GeoJSON object; may not be empty. | ‚Äî |

_definition.ISO_639_3_eng: The shape property for an ordered list of elements of the same type. When empty, the array may contain any number of elements of any type. When non-empty, exactly one shape sub-property defines the element type, and the optional elements property constrains the element count.

_description.ISO_639_3_eng:
An *array* is an ordered sequence of values of the *same type*. Values may repeat; the type definition applies uniformly to every element.

Arrays are *recursive*: the element type is itself a shape property ‚Äî one of [`_scalar`](_scalar.md), [`_array`](_array.md), [`_set`](_set.md), [`_tuple`](_tuple.md), or [`_dict`](_dict.md). Nesting terminates at a [`_scalar`](_scalar.md) or [`_dict`](_dict.md) leaf.

When [`_array`](_array.md) is an *empty object* (`{}`), the array may contain any number of elements of any shape or type, with no further constraints.

When non-empty, exactly *one* shape sub-property must be present. The optional [`_elements`](_elements.md) property may always be added to constrain the minimum and maximum number of items.

_definition.ISO_639_3_eng: The shape property for an unordered list of unique elements of the same comparable type. Unlike other shape properties, [`_set`](_set.md) cannot be empty: the required [`_scalar_set`](_scalar_set.md) property defines the element type, and without it there is no basis for enforcing uniqueness.

_description.ISO_639_3_eng:
A *set* is an unordered collection of *unique* values of the *same comparable type*. Uniqueness requires that elements be comparable, so non-comparable types ‚Äî [`_type_object`](_type_object.md), [`_type_struct`](_type_struct.md), and [`_type_object_GeoJSON`](_type_object_GeoJSON.md) ‚Äî are not permitted as element types.

Unlike [`_array`](_array.md), a set is *not recursive*: its element type is always a scalar, defined by the required [`_scalar_set`](_scalar_set.md) property. An empty [`_set`](_set.md) object is not valid.

[`_scalar_set`](_scalar_set.md) works like [`_scalar`](_scalar.md), but uses [`_type_scalar_set`](_type_scalar_set.md) instead of [`_type_scalar`](_type_scalar.md) to declare the element type. All other properties ‚Äî unit, range, [`_regexp`](_regexp.md), [`_decimals`](_decimals.md), and [`_kind_enum`](_kind_enum.md) ‚Äî behave identically to their counterparts in [`_scalar`](_scalar.md).

The optional [`_elements`](_elements.md) property constrains the minimum and maximum number of items in the set.

_definition.ISO_639_3_eng: The shape property for an ordered list of elements where each position may have a different type. The required [`_type_tuple`](_type_tuple.md) property is an ordered array of descriptor global identifiers; the type at position n is determined by the descriptor at index n.

_description.ISO_639_3_eng:
A *tuple* is an *ordered list* in which each element may have a *different type* depending on its *position*. It differs from [`_array`](_array.md) and [`_set`](_set.md), which apply a single type uniformly to all elements.

The required [`_type_tuple`](_type_tuple.md) property contains an ordered array of descriptor [global identifiers](_gid.md). The type at position *n* is resolved by looking up the descriptor at index *n* and reading its [`_data`](_data.md) section. This indirection means tuple positions reuse existing descriptor definitions rather than duplicating type information inline.

The optional [`_elements`](_elements.md) property constrains how many elements a tuple value may contain. The length of [`_type_tuple`](_type_tuple.md) is the absolute upper bound; neither [`_min-items`](_min-items.md) nor [`_max-items`](_max-items.md) may exceed it. When [`_elements`](_elements.md) is omitted, the tuple must contain exactly as many elements as [`_type_tuple`](_type_tuple.md) has entries. When fewer elements are present, the trailing positions are not applied ‚Äî the tuple is treated as a prefix of the full type sequence.

_definition.ISO_639_3_eng: The shape property for a key/value dictionary with explicitly typed keys and values. Both [`_dict_key`](_dict_key.md) and [`_dict_value`](_dict_value.md) are required; the value side is a full [`_data`](_data.md) section, making the dictionary recursively composable.

_description.ISO_639_3_eng:
A *dictionary* is a key/value structure in which the key type and value type are both explicitly declared. It contrasts with [`_type_struct`](_type_struct.md) (indeterminate properties) and [`_type_object`](_type_object.md) (properties correspond to descriptor [`_gid`](_gid.md)s).

[`_dict_key`](_dict_key.md) defines the key type using [`_dict_key_type`](_dict_key_type.md), which accepts only string-compatible types: strings, enumeration elements, term keys, and document handles. The optional companion [`_kind_enum`](_kind_enum.md) constrains the controlled vocabulary when the key type is [`_type_enum`](_type_enum.md).

[`_dict_value`](_dict_value.md) is equivalent to a full [`_data`](_data.md) section: it contains exactly one shape sub-property ([`_scalar`](_scalar.md), [`_array`](_array.md), [`_set`](_set.md), [`_tuple`](_tuple.md), or [`_dict`](_dict.md)), or is empty to allow any value type. This makes dictionaries fully recursive.

The canonical use case is a *multilingual text property*: keys are language [`_gid`](_gid.md)s (e.g. `ISO_639_3_eng`) and values are plain strings ‚Äî the pattern used throughout the [`_info`](_info.md) section.

_definition.ISO_639_3_eng: A shape property that references a typedef term by key, delegating the data shape definition to that term. Mutually exclusive with the five inline shape properties ([`_scalar`](_scalar.md), [`_array`](_array.md), [`_set`](_set.md), [`_tuple`](_tuple.md), [`_dict`](_dict.md)). The referenced term must carry [`_kind_term_typedef`](_kind_term_typedef.md) and define its shape inline.

_description.ISO_639_3_eng:
[`_type`](_type.md) is the sixth shape option in a [`_data`](_data.md) section. Instead of declaring a data shape inline, the descriptor delegates to an existing typedef term ‚Äî a term that carries [`_kind_term_typedef`](_kind_term_typedef.md) and defines a concrete, reusable shape.

The value is a single `_gid` string that must be the key of a typedef term. During validation, the system performs one lookup: it reads the referenced term's [`_data`](_data.md) section and applies it exactly as if the shape were written inline in the current descriptor. Chaining is not permitted ‚Äî the typedef term itself must use one of the five inline shape properties, not `_type`.

[`_type`](_type.md) is mutually exclusive with `_scalar`, `_array`, `_set`, `_tuple`, and `_dict`. A [`_data`](_data.md) section that uses `_type` must not carry a [`_rule`](_rule.md) section on the same descriptor ‚Äî schema constraints belong on the typedef term itself.

Typical use cases:

- **Reusable multilingual text**: a typedef term `_info_string` defines the dictionary keyed by language `_gid`s; all `_info` property descriptors reference it via `_type`.
- **Shared range structures**: a typedef term defines a range object; multiple descriptors reference it without repeating the key/value structure.
- **Standardised identifiers**: a typedef captures a regexp-constrained string pattern shared by many descriptor terms.

```json
{
	"_data": {
		"_type": "_info_string"
	}
}
```

The validator resolves `_info_string`, reads its `_data._dict` definition, and validates the incoming value against that shape.
